title: Inysh
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:Inysh-TEA.jpg]] |name=Inysh |region=*[[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Inysh system/Legends|Inysh system]] *[[Southern Core]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Inysh system/Legends|Inysh system]] |sector= |system=[[Inysh system/Legends|Inysh system]] |coordinates=J-12 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons= |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial''[[Alien Encounters]]'' |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain= |water= |interest= |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species=[[Abinyshi]] |otherspecies= |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population=At least a few thousand{{JournalCite|14|Alien Encounters (article series)}} |demonym= |cities= |imports= |exports=*Abinyshi [[Foodstuff/Legends|cuisine]] *Abinyshi [[Book/Legends|literature]] *[[Kalonterium]] |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|The Abinyshi have been extinct for decades.''"
"''We have been under the servitude of the Empire. We have been all but wiped out. But a few of us survive, despite Imperial propaganda claiming we destroyed ourselves in some absurd civil conflict.|Tem Eliss and S'itl Thirr|}} '''Inysh''' was a [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to the [[Abinyshi]]. A [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]], passive [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians]], the natives of Inysh were among the first species to achieve [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel, although their [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] [[Colonist/Legends|colonization]] was limited. The [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] eventually discovered the planet had vast reserves of the [[kalonterium]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] and proceeded to strip-[[Mining/Legends|mine]] the world. The ecology of Inysh was devastated as a result, with [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] waste being dumped across the planet and native [[Plant/Legends|flora]] and fauna [[Death/Legends|dying off]]. The Abinyshi were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] and brought to the brink of extinction, and [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] at large was misled to believe the species had eradicated itself in a civil war. At some point, the Empire abandoned the mining operations on Inysh, leaving the natives on their ruined homeworld. ==Description== Inysh was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Inysh system/Legends|Inysh system]], a part of the [[Southern Core]] portion of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. It was [[Homeworld/Legends|home]] to both [[Plant/Legends|plant]] and animal life until [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations carried out by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] led to the collapse of the planet's ecology. ==History== {{Quote|I ask you to write the truth about my people.|S'itl Thirr, to Tem Eliss|}} The native [[Abinyshi]] of Inysh became one of the first [[Species/Legends|species]] to achieve [[Realspace/Legends|space]] travel,{{CSWECite|II|111|Inysh}} taking to the [[Star/Legends|stars]] at approximately the same [[Time/Legends|time]] as [[Human/Legends|Humans]]. However, the [[Reptile/Legends|reptilians']] ability to [[Colonist/Legends|colonize]] territories outside their home [[Star system/Legends|system]] was limited due to their small population. [[File:Abinyshi-AE.png|thumb|left|200px|The Abinyshi were native to Inysh.]] At some point, [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] of the Galactic Empire and officials of the [[Mining Guild/Legends|Mining Guild]] discovered on Inysh the presence of massive deposits of [[kalonterium]], an [[Ore/Legends|ore]] used in weapons manufacturing and [[Starship/Legends|starship]] construction. Subsequently, the Empire established mining operations on the planet and proceeded to strip the world of its kalonterium reserves. As a result, the ecology of Inysh was devastated due to the Empire dumping [[Poison/Legends|toxic]] waste products from the mining process all across the planet, and the world's native flora and fauna [[Death/Legends|died out]]. All but a few thousand Abinyshi were [[Slavery/Legends|enslaved]] to mine kalonterium for the Empire or were otherwise trapped on Inysh, and the species was brought to the brink of extinction. Disinformation due to Imperial [[Propaganda/Legends|propaganda]] led to nearly all beings in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] believing that the natives of the world had been rendered extinct as a result of cataclysmic civil war. When other, higher-quality ores eventually became available elsewhere, the Imperial mining installations on Inysh packed up and left the planet, abandoning the Abinyshi to suffer on their ruined homeworld. Approximately two [[Standard decade/Legends|decades]] later, around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]],The section of {{JournalCite|14|Alien Encounters (article series)}} that describes the meeting of [[S'itl Thirr]] and [[Tem Eliss]] is set shortly after the removal of [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Callow Batta]] from the [[University of Sanbra]]'s [[History Department]]. Furthermore, ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', which states that Batta had been removed "about a [[Standard year/Legends|year]] ago," represents a collection of [[in-universe]] reports from "one year before the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]," which corresponds to [[2 ABY/Legends|2 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, the meeting of Thirr and Eliss can be placed around [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]]. the Abinyshi [[S'itl Thirr]] approached [[Professor/Legends|Professor]] [[Tem Eliss]] of the [[University of Sanbra]]. Thirr requested that Eliss publish the truth about Inysh and its fate. Eliss agreed and eventually included an entry dedicated to the Abinyshi in his work, ''[[The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life]]''. ==Inhabitants== {{Quote|And what ''is'' your species?''"
"''I am Abinyshi.|Tem Eliss and S'itl Thirr|}} Inysh was the homeworld of the Abinyshi, a species of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]] reptilians. The Abinyshi were a passive and gentle culture, and their main exports to the wider [[galactic community]] were their [[Foodstuff/Legends|cuisine]] and [[Book/Legends|literature]]. ==Behind the scenes== Inysh was introduced in an [[August]] [[1997]] article of the "[[Alien Encounters (article series)|Alien Encounters]]" series, which was written by [[Trevor J. Wilson]] and [[Craig Robert Carey]] and included in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 14|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 14]]. The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Inysh system, and therefore the planet Inysh, in grid square J-12. ==Sources== *{{JournalCite|14|Alien Encounters (article series)}} {{1stm}} *''[[Alien Encounters]]'' *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Inysh}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Inysh }} [[Category:Galactic Empire planets]] [[Category:Mining planets]] [[Category:Planets in the Core Worlds]] [[Category:Planets in the Southern Core]] [[Category:Terrestrial planets]]
